title: tmuxcc client → session-proxy messages
description: All control-plane messages sent by the client to the session-proxy. These are input, resize, capabilities, command requests, and resync requests.

ClientCapabilitiesMessage.description: Client capabilities advertisement, sent once at handshake time (step 2 of the server-initiates handshake). Shared between both wires — the same type discriminant is used on both the server-proxy wire and the session-proxy wire. direction: client→session-proxy (and client→server-proxy).

identity.description: Durable client identity presented at handshake (D2, tc-4b6k.1). Additive optional field — a client that does not advertise identity handshakes exactly as before, and an older proxy ignores it. Sibling of `capabilities` (NOT inside the shared Capabilities object, which the server also sends and which has no identity). Both proxies capture it, log it on connect, and surface connected identities in their `*.info` payloads. Carried and logged only in this revision — no behavior change.

InputMessage.description: Client sends text/key input destined for a pane. Input is a UTF-8 string; the session-proxy forwards bytes to the pane's pty without tmux-level interpretation. Special keys should be pre-encoded as byte sequences. Raw byte streams go through the data plane (framing.json), not here. direction: client→session-proxy.

data.description: UTF-8 text to write to the pane's stdin. Not tmux send-keys syntax; raw bytes.

ResizeRequestMessage.description: Client reports a window's viewport (host viewport changed) — tc-cvny. The paneId resolves to a window; the session-proxy reports that window's box per-window (refresh-client -C @<win>:WxH, change-gated) and lets tmux arbitrate natively. For a single-pane window the box is the pane's size; for a strip the extension sends the group's total box here (sash subdivisions go via ResizeManagedWindowCommand). Distinct from ResizePaneCommand (explicit user action). direction: client→session-proxy.

ResyncRequestMessage.description: Client requests that the session-proxy re-send a full snapshot for this connection. Sent when the mirror detects a seq gap. Session-proxy responds with a fresh SnapshotMessage. Added in protocol v2 (tc-7ml.4). direction: client→session-proxy.

PaneAttachMessage.description: Client requests the session-proxy attach (validate + hydrate) a pane on this connection (tc-295a.8). Issued by the broker-mediated first attach (for the transport's primary pane) and by mid-connection bindNew re-hydration. Not correlated — the session-proxy responds with pane.hydration.begin/end on success, or pane.attach.failed{code:"pane.not-found"} on a vanished pane. direction: client→session-proxy.

ClientFocusMessage.description: Client tells the session-proxy that this window gained focus. No payload — the client identity is implicit in the transport (D2). Carries no driver-side sizing effect (tc-cvny): sizing is per-window and owner-less, so focus changes no window's size; the session-proxy accepts and ignores it, and it stays on the wire for the extension's focus tracking. direction: client→session-proxy.

OpenWindowCommand.description: Open a new window in the bound session. The session-proxy chooses the pane id(s); created window/pane ids arrive in the command response.

name.description: Optional name for the new window. If omitted the session-proxy picks one.

cwd.description: Working directory for the first pane (`-c <dir>`). Additive optional. tc-cr4dz.

shellCommand.description: Shell command to run in the first pane. Additive optional. tc-cr4dz.

env.description: Environment variables to inject into the new pane. Each key-value pair is compiled to a repeated -e NAME=value flag on new-window. Floor tmux 3.0 = MINIMUM_TMUX_VERSION; no capability gate needed. Additive optional. tc-gjdx.2.

SplitPaneCommand.description: Split an existing pane into two. The new pane's id arrives in the command response.

paneId.description: The pane to split. When absent, the session-proxy splits the current pane. Additive optional.

cwd.description: Working directory for the new pane. Additive optional. tc-cr4dz.

shellCommand.description: Shell command to run in the new pane. Additive optional. tc-cr4dz.

env.description: Environment variables to inject into the new pane. Each key-value pair is compiled to a repeated -e NAME=value flag on split-window. Floor tmux 3.0 = MINIMUM_TMUX_VERSION; no capability gate needed. Additive optional. tc-gjdx.2.

ClosePaneCommand.description: Close (kill) a pane. The session-proxy emits a pane.closed delta on success.

RenameWindowCommand.description: Rename a window. The session-proxy emits a window.renamed delta on success.

RenameSessionCommand.description: Rename the bound tmux session. The session-proxy issues `rename-session -t =<currentName> <newName>` and the resulting session.renamed delta propagates to all clients. Added tc-6gnc.9.

name.description: The new session name. Must be non-empty.

SelectPaneCommand.description: Focus (select) a pane. The session-proxy emits a focus.changed delta on success.

ResizePaneCommand.description: Resize a pane (explicit user-initiated). Distinct from ResizeRequestMessage (viewport-driven).

ResizeManagedWindowCommand.description: Sash-subdivision push for VS-Code-managed strips (tc-cvny). The session-proxy translates to resize-pane per pane (batched). The window's total box is NOT set here — it is reported per-window via resize.request (refresh-client -C @<win>:WxH); this command only pins the sash subdivisions tmux cannot infer. No window-size manual / resize-window.

cols.description: Window total dims (strip sum, separator-inclusive); reported via resize.request. Carried for the extension's bookkeeping; the session-proxy ignores it here.

panes.description: Per-pane dims for every pane in the strip — the sash subdivisions to pin via resize-pane.

KillSessionCommand.description: Kill the tmux session entirely. Used when killSessionOnLastWindowClose is true. The session-proxy emits ErrorMessage{code:"session.unavailable"}. Uses sessionName (not sessionId) so the session-proxy can pass to kill-session directly (tc-91o).

SetSynchronizePanesCommand.description: Toggle synchronize-panes for a window. The session-proxy emits window.sync.changed delta. Added tc-7xv.12.

on.description: True to enable synchronize-panes; false to disable.

SetMonitorActivityCommand.description: Set monitor-activity for a window. Added tc-7xv.15.

on.description: True to enable monitor-activity; false to disable.

SetMonitorSilenceCommand.description: Set monitor-silence for a window. Added tc-7xv.15.

seconds.description: Silence threshold in seconds (1..N to enable), or 0/null to disable.

BreakPaneCommand.description: Break a pane out of its window into a new window. Corresponds to break-pane -dP. Added tc-7xv.9.

SwapPaneCommand.description: Swap a pane with another. When targetPaneId is absent, rotates with -D (next pane). When supplied, calls swap-pane -s src -t tgt. Added tc-7xv.9.

targetPaneId.description: Optional explicit target pane. When absent, rotates with -D.

RenamePaneCommand.description: Set the DURABLE, driver-owned pane name (tc-1a8z) — the canonical user rename channel. The session-proxy issues set-option -pt %N @tmuxcc_label <name> (the per-pane tmux user-option) and NEVER select-pane -T, so the durable name is a SEPARATE channel from the volatile shell title (pane_title, tc-2mn8) that the shell cannot clobber, and it survives a driver restart. Empty title CLEARS the durable name. The wire kind stays "rename-pane" and the field stays "title" for shape stability, but the SEMANTICS are the durable name, not a tmux pane title. Added tc-7xv.9; repurposed tc-1a8z.

title.description: New durable pane name (stored in the @tmuxcc_label user-option). Empty string clears the durable name.

KillWindowCommand.description: Kill a tmux window and all its panes. Issues kill-window -t @<N>. Added tc-7xv.18.

SwapWindowCommand.description: Swap two windows within the session. Issues swap-window -s @S -t @T. Added tc-7xv.18.

SessionProxyInfoCommand.description: Read-only diagnostics snapshot for the session-proxy (tc-x6l). No side-effects.

PaneCaptureCommand.description: One-shot pane text snapshot (tc-295a.11 / W3.3 / gap A1d). Requests the full scrollback text for a live pane in a single correlated round-trip. Reuses the same capture-pane machinery as the hydration path. Response: command.response { result: { ok: true, payload: { text } } } on success; result.ok=false code="pane.not-found" for a vanished pane (FAIL-LOUD). Extension consumer (E3.2) uses this to kill the C15 out-of-band tmux capture-pane shell-out.

WireCommand.description: Discriminated union of all model-level commands a client may issue. Narrow on `kind`. All commands are model-level — no raw tmux command strings are exposed.

SessionProxyCommandRequestMessage.description: Client issues a model-level command to the session-proxy. direction: client→session-proxy.

correlationId.description: Client-generated opaque string (e.g. UUID), echoed in the matching response. The session-proxy does NOT assign correlation ids.

ClientMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the client sends to the session-proxy. Narrow on `type`.
